info.title: Vertical Farm Ingestion API
info.description:
## Vertical Farm IoT Platform API

This API provides endpoints for:

- **Ingestion**: Receive sensor data from IoT devices
- **Devices**: Digital Twin device registry with real-time state
- **Capabilities**: Master definitions of what sensors can measure (units, ranges)
- **Sensor Types**: Hardware sensor definitions (DHT22, BME280) with capabilities
- **Device Capabilities**: Per-device calibration and configuration
- **Thresholds**: Per-device alerting thresholds
- **Alerts**: Webhook-based alerting system

### Authentication

All endpoints require the `X-Farm-Key` header with a valid API key.

### Capabilities Architecture

```
capabilities (master definitions)
     â
sensor_types (hardware models with accuracy specs)
     â
devices (linked to sensor_type, with calibration overrides)
```

get.description:
Health check endpoint.

Returns API and database connection status. No authentication required.

post.description:
Submit a sensor reading.

**Flow:**
1. Validates device exists (or auto-registers if `auto_register=true`)
2. Updates device state (last_seen_at, current_state)
3. Inserts reading into sensor_readings table
4. Checks configured thresholds and creates `threshold_exceeded` events
5. Queues webhook alerts (async, non-blocking)

**Parameters:**
- `auto_register`: If true, creates device if it doesn't exist. Default: false

**Returns 404** if device not found and auto_register=false.

get.description:
Get the most recent reading for a sensor.

Returns the latest temperature and humidity reading for the specified sensor.

Get reading history for a sensor.

Returns paginated list of readings with optional time range and status filters.

Get aggregated statistics for a sensor.

Returns min/max/avg for temperature and humidity over the specified time window.

**Windows:**
- `1h`, `6h`: Real-time aggregation
- `24h`, `7d`, `30d`: Pre-aggregated for performance

List all devices with optional filters.

Returns device list with summary counts (total, online, offline, unhealthy).

parameters.name: device_type

schema.description: Filter by type: sensor, gateway, controller, actuator
schema.title: Device Type

parameters.description: Filter by type: sensor, gateway, controller, actuator

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by status: online, offline, maintenance, error
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by status: online, offline, maintenance, error

parameters.name: health_below

schema.description: Filter devices with health score below this value
schema.title: Health Below

parameters.description: Filter devices with health score below this value

post.description:
Register a new device.

Creates a device entry in the Digital Twin registry. The device ID must be unique.
After registration, the device can submit sensor readings via `/api/v1/ingest`.

get.description:
List all offline devices.

Convenience endpoint to quickly find devices that haven't reported recently.

List all devices with health score below threshold.

Default threshold is 80. Returns devices sorted by health score (lowest first).

parameters.name: threshold

schema.description: Health score threshold (devices below this are returned)
schema.title: Threshold

parameters.description: Health score threshold (devices below this are returned)

get.description:
Get a single device by ID.

Returns full device details including current state, health score, and configuration.

patch.description:
Update a device.

Partially update device properties. Only provided fields are updated.

delete.description:
Decommission/delete a device.

Removes the device and all associated data (events, thresholds, readings via FK cascade).

post.description:
Send a heartbeat for a device.

Updates the device's last_seen_at timestamp and sets status to online.

Register multiple devices in a single request.

Accepts a list of device objects and creates them all. Skips devices that already exist.

get.description: Get device metadata (config, tags, notes).

patch.description:
Update device metadata.

Merges provided fields into the device's config. Existing keys are overwritten,
new keys are added, unmentioned keys are preserved.

get.description:
Get event history for a device.

Returns device events including status changes, threshold violations, and errors.

parameters.name: event_type

schema.description: Filter by event type: offline, threshold_exceeded, etc.
schema.title: Event Type

parameters.description: Filter by event type: offline, threshold_exceeded, etc.

parameters.name: severity

schema.description: Filter by severity: info, warning, error, critical
schema.title: Severity

parameters.description: Filter by severity: info, warning, error, critical

get.description:
Fetch pending commands for a device (Reader polling endpoint).

**Called by readers** at their poll interval. Atomically marks
commands as 'delivered' when fetched.

Returns commands sorted by priority (lowest first), then creation time.

List all commands for a device (Admin/UI endpoint).

Returns command history with optional status filter.

post.description:
Create a new command for a device.

**Command Types:**
- `config_update`: Update device configuration (poll interval, retries, etc.)
- `restart_service`: Restart the reader service on the device

Commands expire after `ttl_minutes` if not delivered.

patch.description:
Update command status (Reader acknowledgment endpoint).

**Valid Transitions:**
- `delivered` -> `acknowledged`: Device started processing
- `acknowledged` -> `completed`: Execution succeeded
- `acknowledged` -> `failed`: Execution failed
- `delivered` -> `failed`: Immediate failure

Include `result` for success, `error_code`/`error_message` for failure.

delete.description:
Cancel a pending command.

Only commands in 'pending' status can be cancelled.

get.description:
Get all thresholds for a device.

Returns configured alerting thresholds for temperature, humidity, and wifi_rssi.

post.description:
Create a threshold for a device.

Configure min/max thresholds for a metric. When ingest data exceeds threshold,
a `threshold_exceeded` event is created.

**Metrics:** Any numeric capability from the capabilities table (e.g., temperature, humidity, wifi_rssi, co2, ph, ec)

get.description:
Get a specific threshold for a device.

The `metric` is the reading type (e.g., `temperature`), not the unit (e.g., `celsius`).

parameters.name: metric

schema.description: Reading type (not unit). e.g., temperature, humidity, soil_moisture, co2, ph, ec, wifi_rssi
schema.title: Metric

parameters.description: Reading type (not unit). e.g., temperature, humidity, soil_moisture, co2, ph, ec, wifi_rssi

patch.description:
Update a threshold for a device.

The `metric` is the reading type (e.g., `temperature`), not the unit (e.g., `celsius`).
Use `enabled: false` to disable alerting.

parameters.name: metric

schema.description: Reading type (not unit). e.g., temperature, humidity, soil_moisture, co2, ph, ec, wifi_rssi
schema.title: Metric

parameters.description: Reading type (not unit). e.g., temperature, humidity, soil_moisture, co2, ph, ec, wifi_rssi

delete.description:
Delete a threshold for a device.

The `metric` is the reading type (e.g., `temperature`), not the unit (e.g., `celsius`).
Removes the threshold configuration. No more alerts will be generated for this metric.

parameters.name: metric

schema.description: Reading type (not unit). e.g., temperature, humidity, soil_moisture, co2, ph, ec, wifi_rssi
schema.title: Metric

parameters.description: Reading type (not unit). e.g., temperature, humidity, soil_moisture, co2, ph, ec, wifi_rssi

get.description:
List all alert channels (webhooks).

Channels are webhook endpoints that receive alert notifications.

post.description:
Create a new alert channel (webhook).

Configure a webhook endpoint to receive alert notifications.
Supports API key and HMAC authentication.

get.description: Get a single alert channel by ID.

post.description:
Test an alert channel by sending a test webhook.

Sends a test payload to verify webhook connectivity and authentication.

get.description:
List all alert rules.

Rules define which events trigger alerts and which channels receive them.

post.description:
Create a new alert rule.

Rules match events by type and severity, then send to configured channels.
Use cooldown settings to prevent alert storms.

get.description: Get a single alert rule by ID.

patch.description: Update an alert rule. Only provided fields are modified.

get.description:
List alert history.

Returns audit trail of alerts including sent, failed, and suppressed notifications.
Use filters to find specific alerts.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by status: sent, failed, suppressed
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by status: sent, failed, suppressed

get.description:
List all farms.

Farms are the top-level grouping for devices. Each farm has a unique ID
and contains devices at various rack/node/position locations.

post.description:
Create a new farm.

Farms are the top-level entity. Devices are assigned to a farm via `farm_id`.

get.description:
Get a single farm by ID.

Returns farm details including the number of devices registered to it.

patch.description:
Update a farm.

Partially update farm details. Only provided fields are modified.

delete.description:
Delete a farm (smart delete).

- **Hard delete**: If no devices belong to this farm, the row is permanently removed. Subsequent GET returns 404.
- **Soft delete**: If devices exist under this farm, sets `is_active=false` to preserve referential integrity. Use PATCH with `is_active: true` to restore.

get.description:
List all capabilities.

Capabilities define what a sensor/device can measure or control.
Each capability has metadata like units, valid ranges, and display info.

parameters.name: category

schema.description: Filter by category: environmental, network, power, actuator
schema.title: Category

parameters.description: Filter by category: environmental, network, power, actuator

parameters.name: data_type

schema.description: Filter by data type: numeric, boolean, string
schema.title: Data Type

parameters.description: Filter by data type: numeric, boolean, string

post.description:
Create a new capability.

Defines a new sensor/device capability with metadata.
ID must be lowercase with underscores only (e.g., 'soil_ph', 'water_temp').

get.description:
Get a single capability by ID.

Returns detailed information about the capability including units and valid ranges.

patch.description:
Update a capability.

Partially update capability metadata. Only provided fields are modified.
Note: The ID cannot be changed.

delete.description:
Delete a capability (smart delete).

- **Hard delete**: If no sensor types reference this capability, the row is permanently removed from the database. Subsequent GET returns 404.
- **Soft delete**: If sensor types reference this capability (e.g., `temperature` is linked to DHT22, BME280), sets `is_active=false` to preserve referential integrity. The row remains in the database but is marked inactive. Use PATCH with `is_active: true` to restore.

get.description:
List all capability groups.

Returns groups with their member capabilities, ordered by display_order.

post.description:
Create a new capability group.

Optionally include capability_ids to add capabilities to the group on creation.

get.description: Get a capability group by ID with its member capabilities.

delete.description: Delete a capability group (soft delete - sets is_active=false).

post.description: Add a capability to a group.

delete.description: Remove a capability from a group.

get.description:
List all sensor types.

Sensor types define hardware sensor models (DHT22, BME280, etc.) with their
specifications and associated capabilities.

parameters.name: communication

schema.description: Filter by communication protocol: i2c, spi, uart, onewire, analog, digital, modbus, rs485
schema.title: Communication

parameters.description: Filter by communication protocol: i2c, spi, uart, onewire, analog, digital, modbus, rs485

post.description:
Create a new sensor type.

Defines a new hardware sensor model with its specifications.
ID must be lowercase with underscores only (e.g., 'custom_temp_sensor').
Optionally include capabilities to link during creation.

get.description:
Get a single sensor type by ID.

Returns detailed information including all associated capabilities with
accuracy and resolution specifications.

patch.description:
Update a sensor type.

Partially update sensor type metadata. Only provided fields are modified.
Note: The ID cannot be changed. Use capability endpoints to modify capabilities.

delete.description:
Soft delete a sensor type.

Sets is_active=false instead of deleting. This preserves referential integrity
with devices that may reference this sensor type.

Remove a capability from a sensor type.

Unlinks a capability from this sensor type. This does not delete the capability itself.

get.description:
Get resolved capabilities for a device.

Returns capabilities with calibration and overrides applied. Capabilities come from:
- sensor_type: Inherited from linked sensor type
- device_override: Per-device calibration overrides
- legacy: From the legacy capabilities JSONB array

post.description:
Add or create a capability override for a device.

Creates a per-device calibration override for a capability. Use this to:
- Apply calibration offsets/scales to readings
- Set custom min/max ranges
- Disable specific capabilities for this device

patch.description:
Update a device capability override.

Partially update calibration settings. Only provided fields are modified.

delete.description:
Remove a device capability override.

Removes the per-device calibration override. The device will inherit
capability settings from its sensor type (if linked).

get.description:
List all automation rules.

Rules define conditions that trigger commands on target devices based on
sensor readings from source devices.

parameters.name: trigger_on

schema.description: Filter by trigger type: reading, threshold_exceeded
schema.title: Trigger On

parameters.description: Filter by trigger type: reading, threshold_exceeded

post.description:
Create a new automation rule.

Define conditions that trigger commands on target devices when readings
from source devices match the specified criteria.

Example: IF temperature > 30 from sensor-001 THEN send set_state OFF to motor-001

get.description: Get a single automation rule by ID.

patch.description: Update an automation rule. Only provided fields are modified.

get.description:
Get automation system statistics.

Returns counts for rules and 24-hour activity metrics.

List automation history.

Returns audit trail of rule evaluations including triggered actions,
suppressed evaluations, and errors.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by status: evaluated, triggered, commands_created, suppressed, error
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by status: evaluated, triggered, commands_created, suppressed, error

parameters.name: conditions_met

schema.description: Filter by whether conditions were met
schema.title: Conditions Met

parameters.description: Filter by whether conditions were met

AlertHistoryResponse.title: AlertHistoryResponse
AlertHistoryResponse.description: Single alert history entry in response.

AutomationCommandSpec.title: AutomationCommandSpec
AutomationCommandSpec.description: Command specification within an automation rule.

AutomationHistoryResponse.title: AutomationHistoryResponse
AutomationHistoryResponse.description: Single automation history entry in response.

capability_ids.title: Capability Ids
capability_ids.description: List of capability IDs to include

DeviceCapabilityResponse.title: DeviceCapabilityResponse
DeviceCapabilityResponse.description: Single device capability override in response.

farm_id.title: Farm Id
farm_id.description: Farm this device belongs to (must exist in farms table)

child_device_count.title: Child Device Count
child_device_count.description: Number of devices connected to this device

min_value.title: Min Value
min_value.description: Alert if value falls below this

farm_id.title: Farm Id
farm_id.description: Move device to a different farm

ErrorModel.title: ErrorModel
ErrorModel.description: Error information when sensor read fails.

sensor_type.title: Sensor Type
sensor_type.description: Sensor type ID from sensor_types table

IngestPayload.title: IngestPayload
IngestPayload.description:
Payload for POST /api/v1/ingest.
Must conform to Doc 1 - Sensor Data Contract.

ReadingsModel.title: ReadingsModel
ReadingsModel.description: Container for temperature and humidity readings.

SensorTypeCapabilityCreate.title: SensorTypeCapabilityCreate
SensorTypeCapabilityCreate.description: Capability linkage for sensor type creation.

SensorTypeCapabilityResponse.title: SensorTypeCapabilityResponse
SensorTypeCapabilityResponse.description: Capability details within a sensor type response.

TemperatureReading.title: TemperatureReading
TemperatureReading.description: Temperature reading - supports celsius, fahrenheit, kelvin.

tags.name: Ingestion
tags.description: Sensor data ingestion endpoints. Submit readings from IoT devices.

tags.name: Readings
tags.description: Query sensor readings, statistics, and historical data.

tags.name: Devices
tags.description: Device registry (Digital Twin). Register, update, and monitor IoT devices.

tags.name: Device Thresholds
tags.description: Per-device threshold configuration for alerting. Set min/max values for temperature, humidity, etc.

tags.name: Alerts - Channels
tags.description: Webhook channel configuration for alert delivery.

tags.name: Alerts - Rules
tags.description: Alert rule configuration. Define which events trigger alerts.

tags.name: Capabilities
tags.description: Capability definitions with metadata (units, ranges, data types). Master list of what sensors can measure.

tags.name: Sensor Types
tags.description: Sensor type definitions (DHT22, BME280, etc.) with linked capabilities and accuracy specs.

tags.name: Device Capabilities
tags.description: Per-device capability configuration with calibration offsets and custom ranges.

tags.name: Farms
tags.description: Farm management. Farms are the top-level grouping for devices and locations.

tags.name: Device Commands
tags.description: Remote command execution for device management. Send config updates and service restarts to devices.

tags.name: Automation Rules
tags.description: Event-driven automation rules. Trigger commands on target devices based on sensor readings.

tags.name: Automation History
tags.description: Audit trail of automation rule evaluations and command creation.
